programmes.show_description: The Devil's Sweets. 1970. Stars Simon Oates, Maurice Roëves & John Young. A new brand of chocolate has a sinister side-effect. Doomwatch decide to investigate the matter.
1980. Sci-Fi. Two survivors of an alien spaceship crash-land on earth and hold two people hostage. Starring Cameron Mitchell.

programmes.show_name: A Lifetime Ago with The Baim Collection
programmes.show_description: York. 1972. Valentine Dyall tells us about York, an ancient city steeped in history. Whilst its outlook is to the next century, it retains the atmosphere of its heritage.
1963. Who Killed Beau Sparrow? Directed by David McDearmon. Stars Gene Barry, Gary Conway & Regis Toomey. Burke sees artist Beau Sparrow take a hard dive into a pool, a dive which kills him.
1960. Jeb's Daughter. Stars Michael Ansara, John Anderson & Suzanne Lloyd. Sam tries to help a young woman who lives in the backwoods with her father... who has decided to sell her.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life The Flying Soldier
programmes.show_description: 1965. A captivating look at how members of the British army are trained to fly helicopters.
1953. Drama. Directed by Ida Lupino. Stars Joan Fontaine, Ida Lupino & Edmund Gwenn. A man secretly married to two women begins to feel the pressure of his deception.
1959. Drama. Director: Montgomery Tully. Stars Ronald Howard, Carol Marsh & Ian Fleming. After being arrested for robbery and murder, Jenson breaks jail to find the real criminals to turn them in.
1939. Drama. Directed by John Cromwell. Stars Carole Lombard, James Stewart & Charles Coburn. After a whirlwind courtship, a young lawyer and his new wife's love is put to the test.
1965. Comedy. A gang of crooks are caught on a big job. 15 years later they find their loot is now buried under a police station. Stars Sidney James, Sylvia Syms, Dick Emery & Joan Sims.
Number Six. 1962. Directed by Robert Tronson. Stars Ivan Desny, Nadja Regin & Joyce Blair. Charles Valentine discovers that he is being followed by a secret agent from Scotland Yard.
Dead or Alive. 1955. Stars Richard Greene, Victor Woolf, Archie Duncan. Little John, a serf at Rutland Court, is ordered to capture Robin Hood. Discovering the outlaw's camp, John joins the band.
1970. Comedy. Directed by Ralph Thomas. Stars Leslie Phillips, Harry Secombe & Angela Scoular. A British doctor stows away on a cruise liner in pursuit of the woman he plans to propose to.
The Authentic McCoy. 1961. Stars John Ireland, Bill Nagy, Neil Hallett. When penniless Henry McCoy dies, his lawyer discovers he was once rich and plans to get the cash before his son shows up.
1958. Directed by Kenneth Fairbairn. A driver of a coaching tour accounts a wonderful trip across the country with a group of friendly travellers, with glimpses of Stonehenge and the Lake District.
1951. Drama. Director: Maurice Elvey. Stars David Farrar, Geraldine Fitzgerald & Roland Culver. When a Victorian woman dies, a postmortem reveals that her body contains a fatal dose of arsenic.
Person Unknown. 1962. Stars Brian Blessed, Joseph Brady & Leslie Sands. Barlow investigates a serial child molester while PC's Smith and Weir discover a young girl who has been attacked.
1969. Detective & Mystery Drama. Paid In Full. Stars John Baldwin, Alfred Burke & Peter Cellier. When money goes missing, the finger of suspicion is naturally pointed at the ex-convict.
Caroline Munro opens tonight's show with the final episode of Supernatural: Dorabella, a haunting story of capture and escape.
Dorabella. 1977. Stars Jeremy Clyde, David Robb & Ania Marson. A handsome young man tells the story of his escape from a female vampire in Eastern Europe.
Next up at the Cellar Club, Caroline Munro introduces the classic 1960s French horror film, Les Yeux Sans Le Visage.
1960. Horror. Director: Georges Franju. Stars Pierre Brasseur, Alida Valli & Juliette Mayniel. A surgeon causes an accident that disfigures his daughter so he goes to extreme lengths to fix her.
That is all from the Cellar Club tonight and Caroline Munro is here to bring the night to a close.

programmes.show_name: Hammer House of Mystery and Suspense
programmes.show_description: Tennis Court. 1984. Stars Peter Graves & Hannah Gordan. A couple move into a house they have inherited, but it soon becomes apparent that the old tennis court is far from normal.
the Monster. 2021. Documentary. Directed by Thomas Hamilton. Features Ron Perlman, Guillermo del Toro. A look at the life and legacy of Boris Karloff, and the history of the genre he personified.
An insightful documentary by Peter Williams about George Foreman, a man of great faith who was known as one of the best fighters who ever lived, who twice held the World Heavyweight title.
1936. Crime. A financier accepts a bet to spend a night in the Chamber of Horrors. Starring James Carew, Charles Oliver & Lucille Lisle.
Market Day/Minnow Trap/Lobster Boat. 1986. Jack Hargreaves goes shopping at a country market and catches small fish in the nearby stream, before heading out to sea on a lobster trawler.
1954. Crime. Director Terence Fisher and starring John Bentley. Reporters investigate threats against a retired army officer and discover links to a series of murders that occurred during the war.
Winner Take All. 1975. Stars Simon Cuff, Deddie Davies & John Sanderson. Peterkin deserts his master and runs right into the outlaws! Meanwhile, Sister Alice remains the outlaws' prisoner.
1997. Stars Brendan Pollecutt, Fezile Mpela & Robert Finlayson. As Dean and Thabo escape a tribal attack planned by the lord, they continue on their dangerous adventure.
Witches Brew. 1957. Stars William Russell, Cyril Smith & Ronald Leigh-Hunt. A woman with mystical powers convinces King Bors she is working evil. Merlin is enlisted against these powers.
The Wail of the Siren. 1967. Stars Adam West, Burt Ward, Yvonne Craig & Joan Collins. The Siren is launching a two part plan to become the richest and most wicked woman in Gotham City.
The Brothers O'Dell. 1955. Stars Roy Rogers, Trigger and Dale Evans.The O'Dell brothers are at odds when one's outlaw gang robs Mineral City's Wells Fargo office which is managed by the other.
White as Snow. 1967. Stars Donald Gray, Elizabeth Morgan & Ed Bishop. When the Mysterons threaten to kill Colonel White, he tries to leave Cloudbase so the other agents aren't in jeopardy.
The Shrew. 1959. Stars: Conrad Phillips, Willoughby Goddard & Jennifer Jayne. Hedda, lured away by a message that her sister is ill, finds herself an unwilling pawn in Gessler's latest plot.
Mr. Beamish and the Hangman's Noose. 1957. Stars Robert Shaw, Peter Hammond & Paul Hansard. A new governor arrives from England and arrests Beamish. A wary Tempest sets out to find answers.
The Wager. 1956. Starring: Alexander Gauge, Geoffrey Keen & George Rose. Friar Tuck and Robin bet to see who can raise more money. Robin will dress and act as a beggar.
1966. Sci-fi. Directed by Arthur C. Pierce. Stars Wendell Corey, John Agar & Keith Larson. When a starship becomes stranded on an unexplored planet, a mission is launched to save the survivors.
1942. War. Directed by John Farrow. Stars Anna Lee, Paul Muni & Ray Collins. A Norwegian fisherman, enraged at the Nazi atrocities against his village, sets out to seek revenge and restore peace.
Put your dancing shoes on and take a step back in time for Eden Kane's Someone Wants to Know.
The Remittance Man. 1961. Stars Peter Graves, Tony Wickert & Stuart Wagstaff. A family travel to meet their son who was sent to the colonies years ago, but Chris Cobb discovers something shocking.
1962. Drama. Director: Lewis Gilbert. Stars Alec Guinness, Dirk Bogarde & Maurice Denham. During the French Revolutionary War, Captain Crawford is stuck in a battle of with his second in command.
1962. A look at the progress of the European Common Market during a time in which member countries have started to remove trade barriers.
The Fontenay Murders. 1963. Stars Rupert Davies, Edward Chapman & Alan Wheatley. Maigret discovers local prejudices run deep when investigating a series of murders in the town of Fontenay.
A Nice Class of Premises. 1976. Stars Gemma Jones, Donald Burton & Kate Lansbury. All bets are off when HRH becomes His Majesty, King Edward VII. Louisa and Augustus must plan for what lies ahead.
1979. Drama. Director: Harley Cokeliss. Stars Ray Winstone, Julie Shipley & Tony London. A teenager gets out of reform school and heads to Torquay for a seaside holiday with three friends.
1978. Drama. Director: Alan Rudolph. Stars: Geraldine Chaplin, Anthony Perkins & Moses Gunn. Just released from prison, a woman arrives in town to start a new life. Soon starting trouble.
